_spec: the reference operator Ledger Conformance Suite v1.0
_description: Validates double-entry ledger correctness, immutability, and balance invariants.

zero_sum.title: Zero-sum: every transfer creates exactly one DEBIT and one CREDIT of equal amount
zero_sum.description: For each transfer_id, the sum of DEBIT entries plus the sum of CREDIT entries must equal zero when signs are applied (DEBIT negative, CREDIT positive).
zero_sum.enforcement: hard — violation must be rejected at the point of transfer creation

no_negative_balance.title: No wallet may reach a negative balance
no_negative_balance.description: Before committing a transfer, the operator must verify that the sender's balance is >= amount_minor. Insufficient funds must return HTTP 422.
no_negative_balance.enforcement: hard — violation must be rejected at the point of transfer creation

immutability.title: No ledger entry is modified after creation
immutability.description: Ledger entries are append-only. GET /ledger must always return the same entries for a given wallet, with no retroactive edits.
immutability.enforcement: hard — any mutating operation on ledger entries is a protocol violation

trace_propagation.title: All ledger entries for a transfer share the transfer's trace_id
trace_propagation.description: The DEBIT and CREDIT entries for a transfer must carry the same trace_id as the transfer response.

settlement_idempotency.title: Each transfer appears in exactly one settlement batch
settlement_idempotency.description: A transfer must not appear in multiple settlement batches. Operators must track settled transfers and reject re-settlement.

cases.title: Ledger entry must contain all required fields
cases.description: After a transfer, GET /wallets/:id/ledger must return entries with all required fields present.

cases.title: direction must be DEBIT or CREDIT only
cases.description: The direction field must be exactly one of the two canonical values. Any other string is a protocol violation.

cases.title: amount_minor must be a positive integer
cases.description: Ledger amounts are stored in minor units. Zero and negative values are protocol violations.

cases.title: Ledger entries are returned in chronological order
cases.description: GET /wallets/:id/ledger entries must be sorted by created_at ascending (oldest first). This mirrors the event history guarantee.
